transcript.interviewer: Tell me about a time something shifted for you.
transcript.interviewee: Three years ago I was a completely different person. I used to believe that if I just worked hard enough, nothing could touch me. Effort was my whole identity.
I had a failure I couldn't out-work. A project I'd given everything to fell apart anyway. That broke the belief — it was the catalyst, the thing that cracked the old me open.
I'm in the messy middle of it now. I'm learning to ask for help without feeling ashamed, which the old me would never have done. I'm not who I was and I'm not yet who I'm becoming.
transcript.interviewer: Where do you think it's heading?
transcript.interviewee: I think I'm going to come out the other side trusting people more. There's a clear before and after to my life, and I'm somewhere on the journey between them, still moving.
